zh.lifeSummary: 當Dorothy Whitebread Whytebrede於1273年出生在英國艾可懷特 諾特利時，她的父親Sir John Whytebrede I28歲，母親Mrs. John Whytebrede26歲。她和Lord John I Wright, I至少有1個兒子。她去世於1307年在她的家鄉享年34歲。

nameMeanings.name: Whitebread
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Kent and Middlesex): from Middle English whit bred (Old English hwīt brēad ‘white bread’), probably denoting a baker or seller of white bread. White bread, considered the best bread, was made from wheat flour. The name may also derive from Middle English whete bred ‘wheat bread’ (Old English hwǣte brēad), denoting a baker or seller of bread made from wheat. The terms ‘white bread’ and ‘wheat bread’, though of different origin but with identical reference, fell together. Alternatively, the surname may also derive from a metathesized form of Whitberd, from Middle English whīt ‘white’ + berd ‘beard’ (Old English hwīt + beard), a nickname for someone with a white beard.
In some instances, perhaps an Americanized form (translation into English) of the German cognate   Weisbrot  .

variants.name: Weisbrot
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Baruch, Ari, Shalom.  German Erna.

variants.name: Whitmill
meanings: English (Oxfordshire and Northamptonshire): occupational name from the recorded Middle English compound whete-mele, from whete ‘wheat’ + mele ‘meal, flour’, perhaps denoting a miller who ground wheat flour or someone who baked or sold white bread. Compare Whitebread . Alternatively, the surname could be a topographic name from Middle English whit ‘white’ + milne ‘mill’, for a person who lived at a place described or named in this way, but no place with this name has been identified.

nameMeanings.name: Dorothy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

variants.name: Dorothee
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

variants.name: Dorothie
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Dorothy Whitebread Whytebrede was born in 1273, in White Notley, Essex, England, her father, Sir John Whytebrede I, was 28 and her mother, Mrs. John Whytebrede, was 26. She had at least 1 son with Lord John I Wright, I. She died in 1307, in her hometown, at the age of 34.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Dorothy Whitebread Whytebrede est née en 1273, à White Notley, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Whytebrede I, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Mrs. John Whytebrede, avait 26 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Lord John I Wright, I. Elle est décédée en 1307, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 34 ans.
